sentence: Subjective:
The patient, Maria Martin, a 77-year-old female, presents for a follow-up appointment.
She reports being "pretty good" overall, with no specific complaints.
She mentions not increasing her glimepiride as previously instructed, and her sugars are not well controlled, with an A1C of 9.9.
She also reports not eating healthier, consuming 2 slices of wheat bread in the morning, and having a preference for club soda and seltzer water.
She has a history of cataracts, high blood pressure, high cholesterol, and vascular disease in her legs.
She is a smoker and has peripheral neuropathy in her feet.
She reports pain in her hips and upper legs.
Past medical history:
The patient has a history of cataracts, high blood pressure, high cholesterol, and vascular disease in her legs.
She is on metoprolol 50mg once a day, lisinopril 20mg with hydrochlorothiazide 25mg once a day, and amlodipine 10mg once a day.
She is also on atorvastatin, but reports tolerating it poorly due to peripheral neuropathy.
Review of systems:
The patient reports no headaches, dizziness, or shortness of breath.
She has been coughing due to congestion, but the cough is clear.
She has mild peripheral neuropathy in her feet, with decreased sensation in the tips of the first and second toes.
She reports pain in her hips and upper legs.
Current medications:
Metoprolol 50mg once a day
Lisinopril 20mg with hydrochlorothiazide 25mg once a day
Amlodipine 10mg once a day
Atorvastatin (dose not specified)
Glimepiride 2mg twice a day
Metformin (dose not specified)
Objective:
Vital signs:
Blood pressure: 140/60
Pulse: not specified
Oxygen saturation: not specified
Temperature: not specified
Physical exam:
Carotids are normal
Pulse is normal
Heart exam: rhythm is regular, normal rate, S1 and S2 are crisp, no murmurs
Lungs are clear
Extremities are without edema, pulses are palpable on the dorsalis pedis posterior tibial, thickened nails, mildly dry skin, no skin breakdown
Decreased sensation in the tips of the first and second toes
Test Results:
[NOT MENTIONED]
Assessment / Problem List:
The patient has poorly controlled diabetes, high blood pressure, high cholesterol, and vascular disease in her legs, with peripheral neuropathy in her feet.
Status: confirmed.
Problem list: Diabetes mellitus type 2; Hypertension; Hyperlipidemia; Peripheral neuropathy; Vascular disease in legs
Plan:
I prescribed Glimepiride 4mg twice a day to improve blood sugar control.
I instructed the patient to continue her current medications and to monitor for low blood sugars, weakness, shakiness, and sweating.
I will recheck her blood pressure and blood work today, and will see her back in four months.
I encouraged her to quit smoking.
Follow-up:
The patient will return in four months for a follow-up appointment.
